ucs.title: [LLM] BlackCat/ALPHV pre-encryption safe-mode reboot via bcdedit (EDR evasion)
ucs.description: Hunts BlackCat's signature defense-evasion step where the operator reconfigures the boot policy to safe-mode (with networking) via bcdedit and reboots the host so that EDR/AV services do not start, then drops the Rust encryptor. The bcdedit→safeboot→shutdown sequence is a near-pathognomonic ALPHV/Noberus pattern documented by Microsoft, Mandiant, CISA AA23-353A and Sophos X-Ops.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdlines values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("bcdedit.exe","BCDEdit.exe") (Processes.process="*safeboot*" OR Processes.process="*SafeBoot*") (Processes.process="*minimal*" OR Processes.process="*network*") NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer host [ | tstats summariesonly=true count as shutdown_count values(Processes.process) as shutdown_cmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="shutdown.exe" OR Processes.process_name="wininit.exe") (Processes.process="*/r*" OR Processes.process="*restart*") earliest=-30m by host | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` ] | where isnotnull(shutdown_count) | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// BlackCat/ALPHV: bcdedit reconfigures boot to safeboot[network] then host reboot follows
let WindowMin = 30m;
let BcdeditSafe = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("minimal", "network")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"        // exclude machine accounts
    | project BcdTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              BcdCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession;
BcdeditSafe
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName in~ ("shutdown.exe","wininit.exe","wmic.exe")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("/r", "-r ", "restart", "reboot")
    | project RebootTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RebootCmd = ProcessCommandLine
  ) on DeviceId
| where isnull(RebootTime) or (RebootTime between (BcdTime .. BcdTime + WindowMin))
| project BcdTime, DeviceName, AccountName, BcdCmd, Parent, ParentCmd,
          IsRemote, RebootTime, RebootCmd
| order by BcdTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BlackCat/ALPHV: bcdedit safeboot reconfig — Windows 4688 process-create
let WindowMin = 30m;
let BcdeditSafe = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith @"\bcdedit.exe"
    | where CommandLine has "safeboot"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("minimal", "network")
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | project BcdTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName,
              BcdCmd = CommandLine, ParentProcessName;
BcdeditSafe
| join kind=leftouter (
    SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName has_any (@"\shutdown.exe",@"\wininit.exe",@"\wmic.exe")
    | where CommandLine has_any ("/r","-r ","restart","reboot")
    | project RebootTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, RebootCmd = CommandLine
  ) on Computer
| where isnull(RebootTime) or (RebootTime between (BcdTime .. BcdTime + WindowMin))
| project BcdTime, Computer, Account, BcdCmd, ParentProcessName, RebootTime, RebootCmd
| order by BcdTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: BlackCat/ALPHV Safe-Mode Boot Reconfiguration via bcdedit
id: c5d2b9f3-7e1a-4f5d-9c1a-blackcat-safeboot
status: experimental
description: Detects bcdedit.exe configuring the default boot entry to safe-mode (with networking), a hallmark of BlackCat/ALPHV (Noberus) ransomware pre-encryption EDR evasion.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2022/06/13/the-many-lives-of-blackcat-ransomware/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-353a
  - https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2023/07/13/alphv-blackcat-ransomware
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1564.006
  - attack.t1656
  - attack.t1486
  - threat.actor.blackcat
  - threat.actor.alphv
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\bcdedit.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'safeboot'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'minimal'
      - 'network'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - IT-administrator legitimate recovery (rare; pair with shutdown/r within 30 min)
level: high
ucs.rationale: ALPHV/BlackCat is one of the only RaaS crews that operationally relies on rebooting hosts into Windows safe-mode (with networking) to disable Defender/EDR services before detonating the Rust encryptor — Microsoft Threat Intelligence and Sophos X-Ops have repeatedly tied this exact bcdedit→safeboot→reboot→encrypt sequence to Noberus/BlackCat affiliates (and earlier to its progenitor BlackMatter/Dar

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] BlackCat/ALPHV Rust encryptor execution via --access-token / -t argument
ucs.description: Hunts the unique invocation pattern of the BlackCat/Noberus Rust encryptor, which mandates an `--access-token <hex>` (or `-t <hex>`) argument at launch — without it the binary exits. Pairing the token flag with companion BlackCat-only switches (`--paths`, `--propagated`, `--no-prop`, `--safeboot`, `--ui`) yields an actor-specific, low-FP signal even when the operator renames the binary.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process_name) as proc values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process="*--access-token*" OR Processes.process="*-t *") (Processes.process="*--paths*" OR Processes.process="*--propagated*" OR Processes.process="*--no-prop*" OR Processes.process="*--safeboot*" OR Processes.process="*--ui*" OR Processes.process="*--child*" OR Processes.process="*--log-file*") by host Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.user | regex process="(?i)(--access-token|\s-t\s)\s*[A-Fa-f0-9]{32,}" | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// BlackCat/ALPHV (Noberus) Rust encryptor — required access-token argument + companion flags
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
// Token flag — required by ALPHV encryptor (long or short form, ≥32 hex chars)
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(?:--access-token|\s-t)\s+[A-Fa-f0-9]{32,}"
// At least one ALPHV-specific companion switch greatly reduces FPs
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any (
    "--paths", "--propagated", "--no-prop", "--no-prop-servers",
    "--safeboot", "--safeboot-network", "--child", "--ui",
    "--log-file", "--drag-and-drop", "--no-vm-kill")
// Exclude obvious legit tooling that uses --access-token (gh, az, docker, kubectl)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("gh.exe","az.exe","docker.exe","kubectl.exe","git.exe","npm.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          IsRemote = IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession,
          Integrity = ProcessIntegrityLevel
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// BlackCat/ALPHV Rust encryptor invocation — SecurityEvent 4688 (CommandLine auditing required)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(?:--access-token|\s-t)\s+[A-Fa-f0-9]{32,}"
| where CommandLine has_any (
    "--paths","--propagated","--no-prop","--no-prop-servers",
    "--safeboot","--safeboot-network","--child","--ui",
    "--log-file","--drag-and-drop","--no-vm-kill")
| where ParentProcessName !has_any (@"\gh.exe",@"\az.exe",@"\docker.exe",@"\kubectl.exe",@"\git.exe",@"\npm.exe")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: BlackCat/ALPHV Rust Encryptor Command-Line Invocation
id: 7a4f1e62-5d28-4b97-91c2-blackcat-encryptor
status: experimental
description: Detects the BlackCat/ALPHV (Noberus) ransomware encryptor at launch via its required --access-token argument paired with ALPHV-specific operational flags (--paths, --propagated, --safeboot, --ui, --child, --no-prop). Catches the binary even when renamed.
references:
  - https://www.varonis.com/blog/blackcat-ransomware
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-353a
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2022/06/13/the-many-lives-of-blackcat-ransomware/
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/blackcat-ransomware/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/13
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1486
  - attack.t1027
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - threat.actor.blackcat
  - threat.actor.alphv
  - threat.actor.noberus
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  token_flag:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)(?:--access-token|\s-t)\s+[A-Fa-f0-9]{32,}'
  alphv_flag:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '--paths'
      - '--propagated'
      - '--no-prop'
      - '--no-prop-servers'
      - '--safeboot'
      - '--safeboot-network'
      - '--child'
      - '--ui'
      - '--log-file'
      - '--drag-and-drop'
      - '--no-vm-kill'
  filter_legit_tools:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\gh.exe'
      - '\az.exe'
      - '\docker.exe'
      - '\kubectl.exe'
      - '\git.exe'
      - '\npm.exe'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: token_flag and alphv_flag and not (filter_legit_tools or filter_machine)
falsepositives:
  - Custom in-house tooling that accepts a hex --access-token plus a coincidentally-named flag (rare; add an org-specific allow-list)
level: critical
ucs.rationale: Unlike most ransomware families that detonate on a double-click with no arguments, BlackCat/Noberus is engineered as a Rust-based affiliate tool that REQUIRES a per-victim `--access-token` (32+ hex chars) at launch — it will not run without it. This mandatory argument plus the well-documented `--paths`, `--propagated`, `--no-prop`, `--safeboot`, `--ui`, `--child` switches form a command-line finge
